hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/5) affinity by avoiding non-specific interactions in conserved pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy favors strong SSTR2 binding but underemphasizes selectivity. The next iteration should prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved-pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding in SSTR3/SSTR5. Mutating these positions can disrupt conserved-pocket interactions without affecting the pharmacophore core.

Propose mutations at key positions to disrupt off-target interactions. Acidic residues at position 1/2 may form unfavorable interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5, while smaller residues at 5/6/11/12 may reduce hydrophobic contacts.
